answer:
\boxed{C}

This option aligns with research showing that many neurons in the auditory cortex (part of the auditory complex) are tuned to complex, non-pure sounds like speech, rather than simple tones. The fact that 40% of these neurons respond only to complex sounds (e.g., bursts or clicks) suggests specialization for dynamic, speech-like stimuli. Other options contradict known neurophysiological findings about auditory processing.
